     Sources: src/demos/, src/content/components/, src/data/features.ts -->

# Hover Card

A rich preview card that appears when you hover or focus a link — native delays, keyboard support and viewport-aware flipping, all handed to the browser via an interest invoker.

```html
<p class="text-sm text-muted-foreground">
  Follow
  <a
    href="#"
    interestfor="hc-janedoe"
    class="rounded-sm font-medium text-foreground underline decoration-muted-foreground/40 underline-offset-4 transition-colors hover:decoration-foreground focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-ring [anchor-name:--hc-janedoe]"
    >@janedoe</a
  >
  for platform CSS tips.
</p>

<div
  id="hc-janedoe"
  popover="hint"
  class="w-64 translate-y-1 scale-95 rounded-lg border border-border bg-popover p-4 text-popover-foreground opacity-0 shadow-lg transition-[opacity,translate,scale,overlay,display] transition-discrete duration-150 open:translate-y-0 open:scale-100 open:opacity-100 starting:open:translate-y-1 starting:open:scale-95 starting:open:opacity-0 [position-anchor:--hc-janedoe] [position-area:block-end_span-inline-end] [position-try-fallbacks:flip-block] mt-2 origin-top-left"
>
  <div class="flex gap-3">
    <span class="flex size-10 shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded-full bg-primary text-sm font-medium text-primary-foreground">JD</span>
    <div>
      <p class="text-sm leading-none font-semibold text-foreground">Jane Doe</p>
      <p class="mt-1 text-sm text-muted-foreground">@janedoe</p>
    </div>
  </div>
  <p class="mt-3 text-sm text-foreground">Design engineer. Building zero-JS UI and writing about the web platform.</p>
  <p class="mt-3 flex items-center gap-1.5 text-xs text-muted-foreground">
    <svg class="size-3.5" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true">
      <path d="M8 2v4"></path>
      <path d="M16 2v4"></path>
      <rect width="18" height="18" x="3" y="4" rx="2"></rect>
      <path d="M3 10h18"></path>
    </svg>
    Joined March 2024
  </p>
</div>
```

A hover card is a [tooltip](/components/tooltip)'s bigger sibling. A tooltip is a scrap of text on
an icon button; a hover card is a *card* (an avatar, a name, a bio) surfaced from a link in running
text. Rather than wire up hover state, delays and a `pointer-events` bridge by hand, this hands the
whole job to the platform with an interest invoker.

## How it works

- `interestfor` on the link points at the card, a `popover="hint"` element. The browser handles
  showing it on hover **and** keyboard focus, the open/close delays, the top layer, and keeping it
  open while you move the cursor toward it. No `group`/`peer` wiring, no `pointer-events` guard.
- The card declares `[position-anchor:--hc-janedoe]` back at the link's `[anchor-name:--hc-janedoe]`,
  so `[position-area:block-end_span-inline-end]` places it below and to the right.
- `[position-try-fallbacks:flip-block]` keeps it on screen: near the bottom of the viewport the
  browser flips the card above the link on its own.
- The entry/exit animation is the same discrete-transition pattern used across the library.
  `@starting-style` sets the opening frame, and `transition-behavior: allow-discrete` keeps it
  animating out before `display` flips back.

## The fallback story

Interest invokers are Chromium-only for now. In other browsers the card never appears, but the
link still works. If you need hover previews for everyone today, the same idea works with a
`group/card` wrapper and `group-hover`/`group-focus-within` (see the plain-CSS
[tooltip](/components/tooltip) for that technique), though a hand-positioned card can't flip at a
viewport edge.

## Leading-edge features used (and what happens without them)

- **Interest invokers (interestfor + popover="hint")** ([MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Global_attributes/interestfor))
  Without it: The hover UI simply never appears — triggers keep their aria-labels and links still navigate, so nothing is lost. If you need it everywhere today, the plain-CSS technique on the Tooltip page works in every browser.
- **CSS anchor positioning** ([MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/CSS_anchor_positioning))
  Without it: The popover opens centered in the viewport (the top-layer default) instead of attached to its trigger.
- **Entry/exit animations (allow-discrete + @starting-style)** ([MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/transition-behavior))
  Without it: Elements appear and disappear instantly. Nothing breaks — you just lose the animation.
